commandsRun: npm --prefix "D:\coding\somewhat" run test:frontend -- src/App.test.tsx src/editor/EditorView.test.tsx (stdout/stderr captured to overlay/app-flows/focused-vitest.txt)

steps.action: Locate the focused App test and render path for editor mode.
steps.expected: There is direct coverage showing App renders EditorView when the mode store is editor.
steps.observed: App.test.tsx asserts the Open toolbar button is present, and App.tsx returns EditorView when activeMode is editor.

steps.action: Run the focused Vitest command covering App and editor flows.
steps.observed: Vitest exited 0 with src/App.test.tsx and src/editor/EditorView.test.tsx both passing (71 tests total).

steps.action: Cross-check the rendered control against the implementation.
steps.expected: The editor toolbar exposes an Open button through EditorView.
steps.observed: EditorView.tsx renders ToolbarButton labeled Open, matching the App test expectation.

steps.action: Locate the focused App test and teleprompter hint text path.
steps.expected: There is direct coverage showing App renders TeleprompterView when the mode store is teleprompter.
steps.observed: App.test.tsx checks for the teleprompter hint text 'F6 edit · F7 play · Esc exit', and App.tsx renders TeleprompterView when activeMode is teleprompter.

steps.action: Run the focused Vitest command covering App and editor flows.
steps.observed: Vitest exited 0 and the App test file passed.

steps.action: Cross-check the rendered hint against the implementation.
steps.expected: TeleprompterView contains the hotkey hint text used to confirm the teleprompter surface rendered.
steps.observed: TeleprompterView.tsx renders the exact hint text used by the App assertion.

assertions.title: Body CSS class toggles with active mode

steps.action: Locate the focused App tests and body-class implementation.
steps.expected: There is coverage for editor-mode and teleprompter-mode body classes, with no overlap.
steps.observed: App.test.tsx contains one test for initial mode-editor and another for switching to mode-teleprompter while ensuring mode-editor is removed.

steps.action: Run the focused Vitest command covering App and editor flows.
steps.observed: Vitest exited 0 and the App body-class tests passed.

steps.action: Review the corresponding effect in App.
steps.expected: App removes both mode classes then applies only the current mode class.
steps.observed: App.tsx removes mode-editor and mode-teleprompter before adding mode-${activeMode}, matching the contract.

assertions.title: Go Live failure shows alert and stays in editor

steps.action: Locate the focused EditorView failure-path test and implementation.
steps.expected: There is direct coverage for enter_teleprompter_mode rejection showing alert behavior, preserved editor mode, and no hotkey registration.
steps.observed: EditorView.test.tsx contains a dedicated rejection test that asserts alert was called, mode stayed editor, and register_hotkeys was never called.

steps.action: Run the focused Vitest command covering App and editor flows.
steps.expected: The Go Live failure assertion passes.
EditorView should attempt enterTeleprompter, only register hotkeys after success, and alert on failure without changing mode.
steps.observed: EditorView.tsx awaits enterTeleprompter before registerHotkeys, resets playing after success, and alerts in the catch path.

assertions.title: Exit flow unregisters hotkeys, exits teleprompter, resets mode

steps.action: Locate the focused App hotkey-stop exit test and implementation.
steps.expected: There is direct coverage for unregister_hotkeys before exit_teleprompter_mode and resetting playing, editMode, and mode.
steps.observed: App.test.tsx contains a dedicated hotkey://stop test that verifies IPC ordering and final store state reset to editor.

steps.action: Run the focused Vitest command covering App and editor flows.
steps.observed: Vitest exited 0 and the App exit-flow test passed.

steps.action: Review the stop-hotkey handler in App.
steps.expected: App should unregister hotkeys, then exit teleprompter, then force playing=false, editMode=false, mode='editor'.
steps.observed: App.tsx performs unregisterHotkeys, then exitTeleprompter, then resets playing, editMode, and mode to editor.

assertions.title: mode-changed backend event drives App mode switching

steps.action: Locate the focused App mode-changed test plus the event-forwarding implementation.
steps.expected: There is direct coverage showing the backend mode-changed payload updates mode and editMode, backed by an event helper that listens to mode-changed.
steps.observed: App.test.tsx verifies the captured mode-changed listener updates the store, App.tsx wires onModeChanged to setMode/setEditMode, and events.ts forwards e.payload.mode and e.payload.edit from the mode-changed event.

steps.action: Run the focused Vitest command covering App and editor flows.
steps.observed: Vitest exited 0 and the App mode-changed test passed.

steps.expected: The backend event should subscribe to mode-changed and forward both mode and edit into App state updates.
steps.observed: App.tsx and events.ts match the contract: the mode-changed listener forwards both values and App applies them to the store.

frictions.description: The focused file-level Vitest run emitted unrelated React act(...) warnings from other passing EditorView tests in the same file.
frictions.resolution: Treated as non-blocking test noise because the command exited 0 and the assigned cross-flow assertions passed.

frictions.description: The App test file prints an expected stderr log when the unregisterHotkeys rejection-resilience test exercises the catch path.
frictions.resolution: Confirmed the stderr line is expected evidence for a separate passing resilience test and does not invalidate VAL-CROSS-005.

summary: Validated 6 overlay cross-area app-flow assertions with focused Vitest coverage in App.test.tsx and EditorView.test.tsx plus static source reads. VAL-CROSS-001 through VAL-CROSS-006 all passed.
